title: Boat Rental in Tahiti: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
If you are planning a trip to Tahiti, renting a yacht offers the best way to explore its turquoise lagoons and remote motus. From the port of Papeete you can book a motorboat or catamaran for €200, €1,500 per day through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace connecting travellers with local boat owners.

Where to set off in Tahiti

Tahiti has several well-equipped marinas that serve as ideal departure points for day trips and longer charters. Marina Taina in Punaauia is the largest, with deep-water berths and fuel docks. Port d'Arue offers a calm anchorage close to the capital. Papeete Harbour handles inter-island ferries and larger yachts. For exploring the neighbouring island of Moorea, charter operators often depart from Vaiare Bay on the east coast of Moorea, or from the sheltered Cook's Bay on the north shore. Teavaro Pass provides access to the open ocean for experienced skippers.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat (e.g. Axopar 28), ideal for coastal runs and water sports, up to 6 guests, from €350 per day.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), spacious for families or groups, up to 12 guests, from €800 per day.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 65), luxury cruising with crew, up to 10 guests, from €1,500 per day.
  4. Sailing monohull (e.g. Dufour 520), classic experience with moderate comfort, up to 8 guests, from €600 per day.

For a premium motor yacht option, we recommend reviewing the Princess V58 listing on BoatHire24, which includes full specifications and availability.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tahiti?

Rental rates in Tahiti vary by boat type and season, with a full-day motorboat starting around €200.

  • Shared tour (group trip with guide): €100, €150 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 4 guests): €200, €350.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): €700, €1,200.
  • Fully crewed luxury yacht (up to 10 guests): €1,500, €3,000. In the cost section you can also explore our main boat rental Tahiti page for the latest deals and verified operator reviews.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Tahiti?

For powered boats over 6 hp, French regulations require a valid boating licence; foreign licences may be accepted upon confirmation. Most charter operators in Tahiti will confirm the exact requirements when you book through BoatHire24. In 2026, the high season runs from June to October, when the trade winds (known as the Alizés) provide steady sailing conditions from the east.

Tip: In Tahiti, always request a full safety briefing regarding reef navigation and local mooring buoys, as coral protection is strictly enforced by the authorities.

At a glance

  • Price band: €200, €3,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to October
  • Typical trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Max guests (no crew): 6, 12
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Tahiti

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Tahiti?

Yes, you can hire a small outboard motorboat up to 6 hp without a licence, and many operators offer a skipper for larger vessels.

Is it safe to sail from Tahiti to Moorea?

The crossing of about 12 nautical miles is manageable in calm weather, but always check the forecast and ask your charter operator for advice.

What is the best island to visit by boat from Tahiti?

Moorea is the most popular destination, with anchorages in Cook's Bay and Opunohu Bay less than an hour from Papeete.

How do I book a boat in Tahiti through BoatHire24?

Simply visit the BoatHire24 website, search for Tahiti, compare listings with photos and prices, then book online or contact the owner directly.

If you own a boat in Tahiti, you can list it on BoatHire24 and start earning income from your unused days. List your boat today to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 holidays.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tahiti and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
